name: Week 3 Legs (Quads/Hamstrings/Glutes/Calves)
description: Big corrections from W2: leg curl jumps to 90 (real working weight), calf raises bump up, squat starts at 65/side. Walking lunges replace step-ups (back to unilateral basics). You're stronger than you thought.

exercises.notes: Full depth, arms in front for balance
Bodyweight walking lunges, feel the stretch
W2: pyramided to 65/side (175 total) and got 6. Start at 55 for set 1, then 65 for sets 2-4. Goal: 3x6+ at 65/side.
W2: 65/side was hard but doable (8/10/8). Same weight, aim for more consistent reps — 3x10 at 65.
W2: 360x10 all sets, went deeper on last two. Same weight, keep the deeper ROM, push for 12 reps. If 3x12 deep is easy, bump to 400 (4.5 plates/side).
W2: 360 was too easy. Bump to 450 (5 plates/side). Full stretch at bottom, squeeze at top.
W2: 50 was a joke, 90 was real working weight (negatives hard). Start at 90. Make sure the pad position matches what you found in W2. 2-second negatives.
Back to lunges — W1 had these, W2 swapped to step-ups. DBs at sides, 40lbs. Long stride, back knee kisses the floor. Don't rush — your legs are cooked by now and that's the point.

tips: Squat: warm up at 45/side, then 55 for set 1, 65 for sets 2-4. You've earned it.
Leg curl: 90 is your real weight. Don't second-guess it — position matters more than the number on the stack.
Calf raises: bump weight. Calves are stubborn — they need more load, not more reps.
Walking lunges at the end will be brutal on tired legs. That's intentional — time under tension when fatigued = growth.
Rest 3 min between squat/RDL sets, 2 min on leg press, 90s on isolation
If a machine is taken, check Training Notes for swap options
Total volume: ~17 working sets for legs
